payload.text:
Create an app that takes a product image or Logo and visualizes it in various marketing mediums like coffee mugs, billboards, and t-shirts using generative AI Mockups. Ensure product consistency between each frame using nano banana
Add Many customization options including genre, Lighting tone, Color grading, lighting, filters, Image size, Upscaling option, Aspect ratio, and many more.

**Developing the Core Concept**

I'm zeroing in on the core of this "Marketing Mockup Generator" concept. It's becoming clearer: a React web app where users upload a product image/logo, which is then visualized in different marketing scenarios, like coffee mugs. I am also investigating the best ways to source the "mockup" visualizations themselves. I want to build a truly useful tool.


**Refining the AI Integration**

I've been drilling down on the AI integration. My focus now is on using Gemini's image generation capabilities to bring this concept to life. Specifically, I plan to leverage `gemini-2.5-flash-image` to generate those marketing mockup images (billboards, t-shirts, etc.). I want to give users a lot of control – filters, size, aspect ratio, all that. Plus, I'm thinking of adding text-based edits, like "add a retro filter", using the same model, which will be neat.


**Evaluating Model Choices**

I'm now focused on the best model for the task. It seems `gemini-2.5-flash-image` will be my workhorse, as requested for consistent results, especially with "nano banana". However, I'm thinking of adding a "High Quality/Upscale" option using `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`, but only as an optional toggle to maintain the integrity of the original generation request.


**Streamlining Model Usage**

I'm solidifying my approach: simplicity wins. Sticking with `gemini-2.5-flash-image` is the plan for all image generations, keeping things clean and compliant with the "nano banana" requirement. I don't need fancy switches. As for permissions, file uploads are the only necessary request, no camera or mic needed for now unless I add future features.


**Visualizing the UI Layout**

I've been planning out the user interface. There will be a big preview of the generated image, a history gallery underneath showing previous versions, and a download button. I'm imagining it as a clean and intuitive design.

**Defining the Technical Architecture**

I'm structuring the code now. `useState` will manage state for the uploaded image, the generated outputs, and the loading spinner. I need to convert the user's uploaded image to Base64 to communicate with Gemini. I'm building functions: `generateMockup` and `editImage`. I'll engineer detailed prompts based on user input, like "A professional shot of a mug with the provided design..."

**Reinforcing "Nano Banana"**

The user's insistence on "nano banana" consistency is clear. I'll need to guarantee consistency of the products throughout the marketing images with this instruction.


**Constructing Code Modules**

I'm now laying out the core code modules. I'm focusing on the component structure: `ImageUploader`, `ControlPanel`, and `Gallery` components will handle their respective parts. I'm working out the gemini service functions (`generateImage`). I've established the primary data handling in `App.tsx` and am designing a solid data schema.


**Defining Prompt Templates**

My focus is on flexible prompt design. I've created templates like "Create a photorealistic mockup of a {medium} featuring the design from the input image. The design should be naturally applied." I'm adding modifiers for style, lighting, and color, and integrating the user's specific text edits into that. I'm also adding a "Magic Edit" feature for direct instructions from users, giving even more flexibility. I've switched to a dark mode interface with Tailwind colors.


**Adjusting for Model Limits**

I've hit a snag with the `imageSize` configuration: it's not supported by `gemini-2.5-flash-image`, despite the earlier requests. To align with the "nano banana" requirement, I'm removing the `imageSize` dropdown entirely from the interface, for now. Instead, I'll allow users to generate new images with prompts that include terms like "high-resolution" or "4k detail," indirectly scaling the image through the prompt, staying true to our core AI model. I've chosen to focus on providing upscaling cues in prompts for now.


**Initiating Project Structure**

I'm structuring the project in earnest now. I'm building out a solid file layout: essential files include `metadata.json`, `index.html`, and `index.tsx`, as well as `types.ts`, `constants.ts`, and the gemini service functions in `gemini.ts`. I'm also building out my component structure starting with `Header.tsx`, `Sidebar.tsx`, `ImageDropzone.tsx`, and `PreviewArea.tsx`. I'm starting to build `App.tsx` and am starting to wire things up.

entries.description: Root HTML file with Tailwind CSS

export const MEDIUMS = [
  { id: 'coffee-mug', label: 'Coffee Mug' },
  { id: 't-shirt', label: 'T-Shirt Model' },
  { id: 'billboard', label: 'City Billboard' },
  { id: 'tote-bag', label: 'Canvas Tote Bag' },
  { id: 'laptop-sticker', label: 'Laptop Sticker' },
  { id: 'packaging-box', label: 'Product Packaging' },
  { id: 'poster-frame', label: 'Wall Poster' },
  { id: 'phone-case', label: 'Phone Case' },
];

export const GENRES = [
  { id: 'photorealistic', label: 'Photorealistic' },
  { id: 'minimalist', label: 'Minimalist' },
  { id: 'cinematic', label: 'Cinematic' },
  { id: 'retro', label: 'Retro / Vintage' },
  { id: 'cyberpunk', label: 'Cyberpunk' },
  { id: 'studio-glamour', label: 'Studio Glamour' },
];

export const LIGHTING_OPTIONS = [
  { id: 'natural', label: 'Natural Sunlight' },
  { id: 'studio', label: 'Studio Softbox' },
  { id: 'neon', label: 'Neon Lights' },
  { id: 'golden-hour', label: 'Golden Hour' },
  { id: 'dramatic', label: 'Dramatic Shadows' },
];

export const COLOR_GRADING = [
  { id: 'standard', label: 'Standard' },
  { id: 'warm', label: 'Warm Tone' },
  { id: 'cool', label: 'Cool Tone' },
  { id: 'black-and-white', label: 'Black & White' },
  { id: 'vibrant', label: 'Vibrant / Saturated' },
  { id: 'muted', label: 'Muted / Pastel' },
];

export const ASPECT_RATIOS = [
  { id: '1:1', label: 'Square (1:1)' },
  { id: '16:9', label: 'Landscape (16:9)' },
  { id: '9:16', label: 'Portrait (9:16)' },
  { id: '4:3', label: 'Standard (4:3)' },
  { id: '3:4', label: 'Vertical (3:4)' },
];

entries.description: Service to interact with Gemini API
Drag and drop image uploader component
Displays the generated image and gallery
